seo.title: Pennsylvania CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Pennsylvania - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity pennsylvania, how to transfer CNA license to pennsylvania, pennsylvania CNA endorsement application

board.name: Pennsylvania DOH
board.mailing_address: Prometric, PA Nurse Aide Registry, 7941 Corporate Drive, Nottingham, MD 21090

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Pennsylvania.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Nurse Aide Training Program (NATP)
steps.description: Successfully complete a Nurse Aide Training Program (NATP) approved by the Pennsylvania Department of Health. Your training program will provide information on how to register for the competency examination.

steps.title: Register for the Competency Examination
steps.description: Register for the Pennsylvania Nurse Aide Competency Examination through the Prometric Pennsylvania Nurse Aide Registry website. This involves creating a test candidate account with Prometric if you don't already have one.

steps.title: Pass the Competency Examination
steps.description: Pass both the written (or oral) portion and the skills demonstration portion of the Nurse Aide Competency Examination administered by Prometric.

steps.title: Automatic Placement on the Registry
steps.description: Upon successful completion of both components of the Prometric Nurse Aide Competency Examination and meeting all state requirements, your name will be automatically added to the Pennsylvania Nurse Aide Registry by Prometric.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Who administers the Pennsylvania CNA exam?
faqs.answer: The PA nurse aide competency exam is administered by Credentia. Register at credentia.com/test-takers/pa.

faqs.question: How do I transfer my CNA to Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Submit a reciprocity application through Credentia's CNA365 platform online, or mail the NNAAP Registration Application for Enrollment by Reciprocity to: Credentia – PA Nurse Aide Registry, PO Box 13785, Philadelphia, PA 19101-3785.

faqs.question: Is PA CNA reciprocity free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pennsylvania CNA reciprocity is free.

faqs.question: How do I verify a Pennsylvania CNA?
faqs.answer: Use the Credentia CNA365 platform or call Credentia support at (888) 204-6249. You can also call PA DOH at (800) 852-0518.

reciprocity.notes: Pennsylvania handles CNA reciprocity through Credentia's CNA365 platform. Reciprocity applications can be submitted online via CNA365 or by mail. No fee for reciprocity.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in PA.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Pennsylvania.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Pennsylvania
hero.dek: To become a Certified Nursing Assistant in Pennsylvania, applicants must complete an approved training program and pass the competency exam administered by Prometric, who also manages the Pennsylvania Nurse Aide Registry.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Pennsylvania
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Pennsylvania CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: For initial certification, candidates must complete an approved training program and then register and schedule their competency examination directly through the Prometric Pennsylvania Nurse Aide Registry website. For endorsement, applicants download and complete the 'Application for Endorsement' form and mail it to Prometric.
